subtitle: Stories, Survival & Soul
about: Sarajevo is not a city you visit — it is a city that visits you. Over two carefully designed days, you will move through a place where Ottoman bazaars sit beside Austro-Hungarian boulevards, where the scent of Bosnian coffee drifts from doors that have been open for centuries, and where the weight of recent history is carried with remarkable lightness by the people who lived it. | This package is built for the traveller who wants to feel a city rather than photograph it. You will eat with a local family, walk the streets at golden hour, hear the siege explained by someone who remembers it, and leave with the kind of understanding that no guidebook can give you. | Two days. Three tours. One city that will stay with you.

subtitle: Real Bosnia, Deeply Experienced
about: Bosnia is not a small country — it is a layered one. Five days is the minimum time needed to begin understanding it properly. This itinerary moves through three distinct worlds: the Ottoman and Austro-Hungarian complexity of Sarajevo, the raw natural beauty of Herzegovina with its waterfalls and ancient villages, and the surreal Yugoslav legacy of a dictator who built a nuclear bunker in a mountain and thought it would save him. | You will swim in a waterfall, raft a river, taste wine in a medieval cellar, and sit in the bunker of a man who ruled 200 million people. You will eat with a local family on your first night and share a farewell dinner with the same guide on your last. What happens in between is Bosnia — complicated, generous, and impossible to forget. | Five days. Four cities. One country that will permanently change your frame of reference.

day_title: Real Herzegovina — Waterfalls, Wine and Ancient Villages
day_summary: Kravice Waterfalls, Počitelj, wine cellars, Stolac, Blagaj, Mostar.
day_included_activities: Kravice Waterfalls | Počitelj fortress village | Wine cellar tasting & lunch | Blagaj Tekke | Mostar orientation walk
day_optional_activities: Swimming at Kravice (seasonal)
day_morning: Departure from Sarajevo into Herzegovina. The landscape changes within an hour — limestone karst, fig trees, the Mediterranean pushing north. Morning at Kravice Waterfalls, where you can swim in a lake fed by sixteen separate cascades. Then Počitelj — a perfectly preserved Ottoman fortress village that looks like it was painted rather than built.
day_afternoon: Visit to a Herzegovina wine cellar for tasting and lunch. Then Stolac — one of the oldest inhabited places in the Balkans — and Blagaj, where a 600-year-old dervish monastery sits at the mouth of a river that emerges fully-formed from a cliff. Arrival in Mostar around 5pm, hotel check-in, and an orientational walk across the Old Bridge with free time to explore.
day_highlights: Swimming in Kravice Waterfalls | Wine tasting in a Herzegovina cellar | Počitelj, Stolac and Blagaj — three UNESCO-listed sites in one afternoon | First crossing of Stari Most at sunset
day_note: Lunch will be organised within the group by the tour guide and is not included in the package price — you pay directly at the cellar.

day_title: Dictator Tito and Rafting — Wait, What?
day_summary: Tito's Nuclear Bunker, UNESCO Woodcarving Museum, white water rafting.
day_included_activities: Tito's Nuclear Bunker tour | UNESCO Woodcarving Museum | Neretva white water rafting | Farewell dinner in Sarajevo
day_optional_activities: Extra time in Konjic old town
day_morning: Drive to Jablanica, where your guide will introduce the story of Josip Broz Tito before you enter Konjic — a town that somehow contains both a UNESCO-protected woodcarving tradition and a Cold War nuclear bunker built for 350 people that was kept completely secret until 1992. The bunker is one of the most genuinely strange places in Europe.
day_afternoon: White water rafting on the Neretva River — Bosnia's most popular rafting experience, through a canyon that gets progressively more dramatic. Lunch on the riverbank. Then the drive back to Sarajevo and a farewell dinner with your guide — the best ending to five days that went by far too quickly.
day_highlights: Tito's Nuclear Bunker — Cold War history at its most surreal | UNESCO Woodcarving Museum in Konjic | White water rafting on the Neretva River | Farewell dinner in Sarajevo

subtitle: Empires, Mountains & the Adriatic Coast
about: This journey connects two of the most dramatic cities in the Balkans — Sarajevo, the city where East met West and history turned on a bullet, and Dubrovnik, the walled pearl of the Adriatic. In between lies a landscape most travellers never see: Herzegovina's limestone gorges, Ottoman bridge towns, the wild Neretva river, and a short but sovereign corridor of Bosnian sea. | The route is not just a transfer — it is a narrative. You will move through layers of Ottoman, Austro-Hungarian, Yugoslav, and Mediterranean history. You will eat burek in a Sarajevo backstreet, swim beneath the old bridge in Mostar at golden hour, taste Herzegovinian wine in a family cellar, and walk the walls of Dubrovnik as the Adriatic turns orange below you. | Seven days. day_summary: Arrival, orientation walk, and first taste of Bosnian hospitality.
day_included_activities: Private arrival transfer | Orientation walk through Baščaršija | Welcome dinner at local konoba
day_optional_activities: Evening free walk in the old town
day_morning: Arrival into Sarajevo by air or road. Private transfer to your accommodation in the old town or immediate surroundings. Settle in, freshen up, and step outside — the city begins at your doorstep. Baščaršija, the Ottoman bazaar quarter, is within walking distance. Let it disorient you gently. That is part of the design.
day_afternoon: At 16:00 meet your guide for a 90-minute orientation walk through Baščaršija and across the invisible line where the Ottoman city meets the Austro-Hungarian boulevard. Your guide will explain why this 200-metre stretch is called the 'Jerusalem of Europe'. Dinner is at a local konoba — slow-roasted lamb under the sač, Bosnian bread, and your first glass of Žilavka white wine. Evening free.
day_highlights: Arrival private transfer | Orientation walk through Baščaršija | Ottoman-to-Austro-Hungarian city transition point | First Bosnian dinner at a local konoba
day_note: If arriving late, the orientation walk moves to Day 2 morning. Dinner still happens.

day_summary: Full day in Sarajevo — history, siege, coffee ritual, and local lunch.
day_included_activities: Bosnian coffee ceremony | Between Empires Walking Tour | Siege of Sarajevo Tour | Local neighbourhood lunch
day_optional_activities: Tunnel of Hope Museum | War Childhood Museum | Rooftop group dinner
day_morning: 09:00 Bosnian coffee ceremony at a traditional kafana. 09:45 Begin the Between Empires walking tour: Latin Bridge (where Franz Ferdinand was shot), Gazi Husrev-beg Mosque, the Old Orthodox Church, the Sephardic synagogue — four religions within 500 metres. 11:30 Siege of Sarajevo tour begins.
day_afternoon: 13:30 Lunch at a neighbourhood restaurant your guide knows. 15:30 Free afternoon. Options: Tunnel of Hope Museum, the War Childhood Museum, copper shopping in the čaršija, or simply sitting by the Miljacka river. 19:00 Optional group dinner at a rooftop restaurant.
day_highlights: Bosnian coffee ceremony | Between Empires walking tour | Siege of Sarajevo interpretation | Local lunch away from tourist circuits | Free afternoon exploration
day_note: The siege content is emotionally heavy. Guests who are sensitive should be prepared. The guide handles it with care — but does not sanitise it.

day_summary: Drive south through the Neretva canyon, Tito's bunker, and the wartime bridge.
day_included_activities: Neretva canyon drive | Konjic Ottoman bridge | ARK D-0 bunker tour | Jablanica riverside lamb lunch | Stari Most sunset walk
day_optional_activities: Mostar evening old town walk
day_morning: 09:00 Depart Sarajevo by private minibus. The road south descends almost immediately into the Neretva river gorge — one of the most dramatic drives in the western Balkans. First stop: Konjic. A small Ottoman bridge town with a 17th-century stone arch bridge and a woodcarving tradition still practiced today.
day_afternoon: 11:30 ARK D-0 — Tito's nuclear bunker. Carved into the mountain above Konjic between 1953 and 1979, designed to shelter Yugoslav leadership through a nuclear war. Guided tour inside the bunker: 90 minutes. 13:30 Lunch at Jablanica — famous throughout former Yugoslavia for its lamb roasted over open fire beside the river. 15:30 Continue south toward Mostar. Arrive late afternoon.
day_highlights: Neretva canyon drive | Konjic Ottoman bridge | ARK D-0 — Tito's nuclear bunker | Jablanica spit-roast lamb lunch | First view of Mostar's Old Bridge at dusk
day_note: The bunker tour requires booking in advance. Confirm at least 48 hours before departure.

day_summary: Full day in Mostar — the bridge, the war, the old town, and a swim.
day_included_activities: Stari Most early morning walk | Mostar war history walk | Local trout lunch | Blagaj Tekke visit
day_optional_activities: Bridge diver watch | Swimming under Stari Most (June–Sep)
day_morning: 08:30 Morning walk to Stari Most before the tourist crowds arrive. Your guide explains the construction of the original 16th-century Ottoman bridge, its destruction in 1993 by artillery, and its meticulous reconstruction completed in 2004. 10:00 War history walk through Mostar — the front line ran through this city for years.
day_afternoon: 12:30 Lunch at a family-run restaurant on the eastern bank — grilled trout from the Neretva, Herzegovinian salad, local bread. 14:30 Optional: watch a bridge diver from the Stari Most diving club prepare and jump. 15:30 Drive 20 minutes to the Blagaj Tekke — a 16th-century Dervish monastery built into a cliff face at the source of the Buna river.
day_highlights: Early morning Stari Most walk | War history walk through divided Mostar | Local trout lunch on the Neretva | Bridge divers — real tradition, not a show | Blagaj Tekke cliff monastery
day_note: Swimming beneath the Old Bridge is possible June–September. Water temperature and safety conditions permitting.

day_summary: Herzegovinian wine cellars, Bosnia's only sea, and the Pelješac peninsula.
day_meals: Wine tasting & food pairing | Neum seafood lunch
day_included_activities: Herzegovinian wine cellar tasting | Neum coastal stop | Mali Ston oyster tasting
day_optional_activities: Sunset walk on Stradun
day_morning: 09:00 Depart Mostar. Drive south through the Herzegovinian wine country. Visit a family winery in the Čapljina area. Not a commercial tasting room — a working cellar with a family who has been making wine for three generations. Guided tasting of 4–5 wines with local cheese and dried meats.
day_afternoon: 11:30 Continue south to Neum — Bosnia and Herzegovina's only coastal town. Lunch at a seafood restaurant on the waterfront. 13:30 Cross into Croatia. Drive the Pelješac peninsula. Stop at Mali Ston for the famous oysters. Arrive Dubrovnik late afternoon.
day_highlights: Herzegovinian family wine cellar tasting | Neum — Bosnia's only sea access | Pelješac peninsula coastal drive | Mali Ston oysters | Arrival into Dubrovnik
day_note: The winery visit is at a working family farm — not a polished tour operator. That is the point.

day_summary: Old town immersion, city walls walk, and the real Dubrovnik beneath the tourism.
day_included_activities: City walls circuit | Old town guided tour | Local restaurant lunch
day_optional_activities: Lokrum island ferry | Mount Srđ cable car | Sea kayaking around the walls
day_morning: 08:00 Enter the old town before the cruise ship crowds arrive. Your guide begins at the Pile Gate. Walk the Stradun, enter the Rector's Palace, find the streets that do not appear on the standard tour. 09:30 City walls walk — 2km circuit around the medieval fortifications.
day_afternoon: 12:00 Lunch at a restaurant inside the old town chosen for quality rather than location. 14:00 Free afternoon. Options: Lokrum island ferry, cable car to Mount Srđ, sea kayaking around the walls, or simply sitting in a small square with a coffee. 19:30 Sunset drinks on a terrace overlooking the Adriatic.
day_highlights: Early morning old town entry before crowds | City walls circuit — 2km walk | 1991 siege damage visible in the roof tiles | Lokrum island, cable car, or kayaking options | Sunset over the Adriatic
day_note: July and August crowds inside the walls are significant. The early morning start on Day 6 is deliberate — non-negotiable for the best experience.

day_summary: Final morning in the old town, farewell breakfast, transfer to airport.
day_included_activities: Farewell breakfast | Private airport transfer
day_optional_activities: Final old town morning walk
day_morning: Final morning is structured around your departure time. If you leave after midday, there is time for one more walk. Farewell breakfast at a café your guide considers the best morning spot in Dubrovnik. Pastries, local honey, strong coffee.
day_afternoon: Private transfer to Dubrovnik Airport (Čilipi), approximately 25 minutes from the old town. For guests with late afternoon or evening flights: luggage storage arranged, full free day in Dubrovnik until transfer time.
day_highlights: Final old town morning walk | Farewell breakfast at a local café | Luggage storage for late departures | Private airport transfer
day_note: Dubrovnik Airport is small and can be congested in peak season. Allow 2 hours before departure.

day_summary: Arrival day with welcome dinner at a Bosnian aščinica.
day_included_activities: Private arrival transfer | Welcome dinner at aščinica | Tour leader briefing
day_optional_activities: Evening Baščaršija walk
day_morning: Private airport transfers to your hotel in the Old Town. Time to settle in and orient yourself in the city at your own pace.
day_afternoon: Free afternoon to walk the Baščaršija and find your bearings. Optional coffee at one of the historic coffeehouses. Welcome briefing at 18:30 followed by a welcome dinner at a traditional aščinica — slow-cooked Bosnian dishes: begova čorba, dolma, ćevapi.
day_highlights: Welcome dinner with the group | First evening in the Baščaršija old town | Tour leader introduction and trip briefing
day_note: Arrival times are flexible. Welcome dinner at 19:30 is the only fixed point.

day_summary: Bosnian coffee ritual and the civilizations walking tour.
day_included_activities: Bosnian coffee ceremony | Between Empires Walking Tour | City Hall (Vijećnica)
day_optional_activities: Yellow Fortress sunset climb | Kafana sevdah dinner — €60
day_morning: Bosnian coffee ritual at a Baščaršija coffeehouse. Walking tour through the 200-metre civilizations corridor: Sebilj fountain → Gazi Husrev-beg mosque → Sacred Heart Cathedral. Latin Bridge — the assassination that ended the 19th century.
day_afternoon: Lunch break (own arrangement). City Hall (Vijećnica) — burned 1992, rebuilt 2014 — and the Sarajevo Haggadah story. Free evening. Optional climb to the yellow fortress (Žuta Tabija) for sunset over the city.
day_highlights: Bosnian coffee ceremony — the correct way | Ottoman to Austro-Hungarian in 200 metres | Latin Bridge and the assassination of 1914 | City Hall and the Sarajevo Haggadah
day_note: Long walking day on cobblestones — wear proper shoes.

day_summary: Tunnel of Hope and Trebević mountain — the hardest emotional day.
day_included_activities: Tunnel of Hope guided tour | Trebević mountain drive | Olympic bobsled track
day_optional_activities: Gallery 11/07/95 (Srebrenica) | Sevdah music dinner — €60
day_morning: Tunnel of Hope (Tunel Spasa) — guided by a former defender or family member. The 800-metre wartime lifeline that kept Sarajevo alive from 1992 to 1995.
day_afternoon: Drive up Trebević mountain to the Olympic bobsled track — now a graffiti corridor through the forest. Cable car back down. Free time for Gallery 11/07/95 (Srebrenica content — emotionally heavy, optional) or copper workshops in Kazandžiluk. Optional group dinner at a kafana with sevdah live music — €60 supplement.
day_highlights: Tunnel of Hope guided by someone who lived it | Trebević Olympic bobsled track | Optional sevdah music dinner
day_note: Most emotionally demanding day of the journey. Opt-out available for any segment.

day_summary: Cross into Serbia via the UNESCO Drina bridge from Andrić's novel.
day_included_activities: Višegrad Andrić's Bridge | Reading from the novel on site | Andrićgrad walk
day_optional_activities: Skadarlija evening (own arrangement)
day_morning: 08:00 departure east through the Drina valley. 11:00 arrival at Višegrad — the Mehmed Paša Sokolović Bridge (UNESCO, 1577). Reading aloud: the opening passage of Ivo Andrić's The Bridge on the Drina, on the bridge itself.
day_afternoon: Lunch in Višegrad. Walk through Andrićgrad. 14:00 cross into Serbia at Kotroman/Vardište border. Arrive Belgrade by 17:30. Walk to Skadarlija — the bohemian quarter — for dinner (own arrangement).
day_highlights: Andrić's bridge at Višegrad | Reading from The Bridge on the Drina on site | First border crossing into Serbia | Skadarlija bohemian quarter
day_note: Long transit day. Border crossing can add 30–60 minutes in summer.

day_summary: Drive south through Serbia plus the figure-8 mountain railway.
day_included_activities: Sirogojno ethno-village | Šargan 8 railway (pre-booked) | Drvengrad walk
day_morning: 09:00 departure southwest. 13:00 arrival at Sirogojno open-air ethno-village — wooden houses, traditional cuisine: komplet lepinja, smoked pršut.
day_afternoon: 15:30 Mokra Gora — ride the Šargan 8 narrow-gauge railway. A figure-8 climb through 22 tunnels, originally built in 1921 to connect Sarajevo and Belgrade. Optional walk through Drvengrad. Dinner and overnight at a mountain hotel in Zlatibor.
day_highlights: Sirogojno ethno-village lunch | Šargan 8 figure-8 climb through 22 tunnels | Drvengrad wooden village
day_note: Šargan 8 has fixed daily departures (typically 10:30 and 13:25) and sells out in summer. Pre-booked.

day_summary: The White Angel fresco then up to the highest town in the Balkans.
day_included_activities: Mileševa Monastery & White Angel fresco | Montenegro border crossing | Arrival at Žabljak (1,450m)
day_morning: 08:30 departure south through the Sandžak. 10:00 Mileševa Monastery (13th century) — the White Angel fresco. Used in the first satellite TV transmission from Europe to America in 1962.
day_afternoon: 12:00 cross into Montenegro. 14:00 lunch in Pljevlja. 17:00 arrive Žabljak (1,450m) — the highest town in the Balkans. Dinner at the hotel. Early night — altitude and tomorrow is active.
day_highlights: Mileševa Monastery and the White Angel | Crossing the Sandžak region | Second border crossing into Montenegro | Arrival at the highest town in the Balkans
day_note: Long transit day. Altitude at Žabljak (1,450m) — drink water, ease into the evening.

day_summary: Black Lake morning and choice afternoon with optional rafting.
day_included_activities: Black Lake walk (3.7 km) | Group sač lamb dinner
day_optional_activities: Tara Canyon rafting — €55 | Đurđevića bridge zipline | Curevac viewpoint hike
day_morning: 09:00 Black Lake (Crno jezero) — easy 3.7 km loop walk. The signature glacial lake of Durmitor.
day_afternoon: 12:00 lunch at the lake or in town. Choice afternoon: (a) Tara Canyon rafting, Class II–III, 18 km, approx. 3 hrs on water — €55 supplement; (b) Đurđevića Tara bridge viewpoint and zipline; (c) Curevac viewpoint hike, moderate, 2 hrs. 20:00 group dinner — local lamb cooked under the sač.
day_highlights: Black Lake morning walk | Optional Tara Canyon rafting | Sač lamb dinner — a regional specialty
day_note: Tara rafting cancels in heavy rain — backup is the Đurđevića viewpoint and zipline.

day_summary: The Piva canyon descent and Ostrog cliff monastery.
day_included_activities: Piva canyon drive (56 tunnels) | Ostrog Monastery guided visit
day_optional_activities: Kotor evening old town walk
day_morning: 09:00 descent through the Piva canyon — one of the most spectacular drives in Europe: narrow road, 56 tunnels, sheer walls dropping to the river below.
day_afternoon: 12:30 Ostrog Monastery — built into a vertical cliff face. The most important pilgrimage site in Montenegro. 14:00 lunch in Nikšić. 17:30 arrive Kotor. Walk into the walled old town. Free evening to settle in and dine.
day_highlights: Piva canyon scenic drive — 56 tunnels | Ostrog cliff monastery | First evening in Kotor's walled old town
day_note: Quiet, respectful behaviour at Ostrog. Modest dress required.

day_summary: The walls climb at first light then a boat tour of the bay.
day_included_activities: Saint John's fortress climb | Bay of Kotor boat tour | Our Lady of the Rocks | Old town walking tour
day_optional_activities: Cathedral of Saint Tryphon interior | Maritime Museum
day_morning: 08:30 climb the city walls to Saint John's fortress — 1,355 steps, 260m elevation. Best done early before the heat. Half-way option available.
day_afternoon: 11:00 boat tour of the Bay of Kotor — Perast and Our Lady of the Rocks, the man-made island built by sailors stone by stone over 200 years. Light lunch in Perast. 15:30 return to Kotor. Old town walking tour: Cathedral of Saint Tryphon (1166), Maritime Museum, the Venetian lions on every gate.
day_highlights: Saint John's fortress walls climb — 1,355 steps | Bay of Kotor boat tour | Our Lady of the Rocks story | Cathedral of Saint Tryphon (1166)
day_note: Wall climb in heat is dangerous — early start non-negotiable in summer.

day_summary: Twenty-five serpentines to the Njegoš mausoleum then south to Dubrovnik.
day_included_activities: Lovćen National Park & serpentines | Njegoš Mausoleum (1,657m) | Sveti Stefan photo stop
day_optional_activities: Budva old town walk
day_morning: 09:00 drive the 25 hairpin serpentines up to Lovćen National Park. 10:30 Njegoš Mausoleum at the summit (1,657m) — the resting place of the poet-prince-bishop who is to Montenegro what Shakespeare is to England. 461 steps to the top.
day_afternoon: 13:00 lunch in Cetinje. 15:00 photo stop at Sveti Stefan and Budva. 17:30 cross into Croatia. 19:00 arrive Dubrovnik. Free evening.
day_highlights: Lovćen 25 serpentines drive | Njegoš Mausoleum at 1,657m | Sveti Stefan photo stop | Third border crossing into Croatia
day_note: Serpentines plus motion sickness — sit forward, ginger candy onboard.

day_summary: The walls at first light and a free afternoon for Lokrum or Srđ.
day_included_activities: City walls circuit (1.94 km) | Old town walking tour
day_optional_activities: Lokrum island ferry | Mount Srđ cable car | Sea kayaking
day_morning: 08:00 walk the city walls — 1.94 km circuit. Early start mandatory in summer; by 10:00 the cruise crowds make it unpleasant.
day_afternoon: 10:30 old town walking tour — Stradun, Onofrio's fountains, Rector's Palace, Franciscan monastery (third-oldest pharmacy in Europe, est. 1317). Free afternoon: ferry to Lokrum island or cable car up Srđ for sunset.
day_highlights: Dubrovnik walls at first light — 1.94 km circuit | Stradun and the Rector's Palace | Free afternoon: Lokrum or Srđ sunset
day_note: Walls in summer heat — early start essential. Bring water.

day_summary: Ston walls, oysters, and Plavac Mali wine then Diocletian's Palace.
day_meals: Breakfast | Wine tasting & food pairing | Ston oysters
day_included_activities: Ston defensive walls | Pelješac Plavac Mali wine tasting | Diocletian's Palace guided tour
day_optional_activities: Split Riva evening walk
day_morning: 09:00 departure north along the Adriatic. 10:30 Ston — the 5.5 km of medieval defensive walls with oysters from the bay below.
day_afternoon: 12:30 Pelješac peninsula wine tasting — Plavac Mali, the Croatian parent grape of Zinfandel and Primitivo. Family winery in the Dingač or Postup region. 15:30 continue to Split. 18:00 arrive Split. Guided walk through Diocletian's Palace. Free evening on the Riva.
day_highlights: Ston defensive walls and oysters | Pelješac Plavac Mali wine tasting | Diocletian's Palace walking tour
day_note: Wine tasting is included with food pairing.

day_summary: Počitelj cliff fortress, Blagaj Sufi tekija, and the rebuilt Mostar bridge.
day_included_activities: Počitelj cliff fortress | Blagaj Tekija | Stari Most guided tour | Farewell riverside dinner
day_optional_activities: Bridge diver watch — €25
day_morning: 09:30 departure south. Cross back into Bosnia — fourth border. 12:00 Počitelj — Ottoman fortress town stacked on a cliff above the Neretva.
day_afternoon: 14:30 Blagaj Tekija — a 16th-century Sufi dervish house at the source of the Buna river, where water emerges from a cliff at 43 m³/sec. 16:30 arrive Mostar. Walking tour of Stari Most — built 1566, destroyed 1993, rebuilt 2004. 20:00 farewell dinner — Herzegovinian wine and japrak in a riverside restaurant overlooking the bridge lit up at night.
day_highlights: Počitelj cliff fortress climb | Blagaj Sufi tekija and the river source | Stari Most walking tour | Farewell dinner over the lit bridge
day_note: Final group night. Bridge divers only jump when paid (€25 each).

subtitle: Sarajevo, Višegrad & Belgrade in 5 Days
about: From the Ottoman bazaars of Sarajevo to the Habsburg boulevards a hundred metres away, this 5-day journey is built around one question: how does a place hold so many empires at once? You will walk Sarajevo's civilizations corridor, taste Bosnian coffee the way it is meant to be tasted, and hear the siege explained by someone who lived it. | Then east — across the Drina to Andrić's UNESCO bridge, into Serbia, and up to Belgrade where two great rivers meet at a fortress that has been fought over for 2,300 years. The Sava and the Danube. The Balkans literally begin here. | Five days. Two countries. One coherent story about the edge between worlds.

subtitle: Sarajevo, Višegrad & Belgrade in 5 Days
about: From the Ottoman bazaars of Sarajevo to the Habsburg boulevards a hundred metres away, this 5-day journey is built around one question: how does a place hold so many empires at once? You will walk Sarajevo's civilizations corridor, taste Bosnian coffee the way it is meant to be tasted, and hear the siege explained by someone who lived it. | Then east — across the Drina to Andrić's UNESCO bridge, into Serbia, and up to Belgrade where two great rivers meet at a fortress that has been fought over for 2,300 years. The Sava and the Danube. The Balkans literally begin here. | Five days. Two countries. One coherent story about the edge between worlds.

day_summary: Coffee ritual and the civilizations walking tour.
day_included_activities: Bosnian coffee ceremony | Between Empires Walking Tour | City Hall (Vijećnica)
day_optional_activities: Tunnel of Hope Museum — €15 | Yellow Fortress walk
day_morning: Bosnian coffee ritual at a Baščaršija coffeehouse — explained as ceremony, not caffeine. Walking tour through the 200-metre civilizations corridor: Sebilj fountain to Gazi Husrev-beg mosque to Sacred Heart Cathedral. Latin Bridge — the assassination that ended the 19th century.
day_afternoon: Lunch break (own arrangement). City Hall (Vijećnica) — burned 1992, rebuilt 2014 — and the Sarajevo Haggadah story.
day_highlights: Bosnian coffee ceremony | Ottoman to Austro-Hungarian in 200 metres | Latin Bridge and the assassination of 1914 | City Hall and the Sarajevo Haggadah
day_note: Long walking day on cobblestones — wear proper shoes.

day_morning: Tunnel of Hope guided by a former defender. The 800-metre wartime lifeline that kept Sarajevo alive 1992–95.

day_highlights: Tunnel of Hope guided by someone who lived it | Trebević Olympic bobsled track | Optional Srebrenica gallery (emotionally heavy)
day_note: Most emotionally demanding day. Opt-out available for any segment.

subtitle: Belgrade to Kotor through Durmitor in 5 Days
about: Most travellers cross from Serbia to Montenegro in a hurry. This 5-day journey takes the long way — through the Sandžak, past the 13th-century White Angel fresco, into the highest town in the Balkans, and finally down through the Piva canyon to the walls of Kotor. | You will ride the Šargan 8 figure-8 mountain railway, walk the Black Lake at Durmitor, optionally raft the deepest canyon in Europe, and stand at Ostrog where a monastery has been built into a vertical cliff face. The Adriatic at the end is your reward. | Five days. Two countries. The mountains most operators skip.

day_summary: Drive south through Serbia plus the figure-8 mountain railway.

day_morning: 09:00 departure southwest. 13:00 arrival at Sirogojno open-air ethno-village — wooden houses and traditional cuisine: komplet lepinja, smoked pršut.

day_note: Šargan 8 is pre-booked — train departures are fixed.

day_title: White Angel to the Highest Town
day_summary: The Mileševa fresco then up to Žabljak at 1,450m.
day_included_activities: Mileševa Monastery & White Angel fresco | Montenegro border crossing
day_morning: 08:30 departure south through the Sandžak. 10:00 Mileševa Monastery — the White Angel fresco. One of the masterpieces of European medieval painting; used in the first satellite TV transmission to America in 1962.
day_afternoon: 12:00 cross into Montenegro. 14:00 lunch in Pljevlja. 17:00 arrive Žabljak — the highest town in the Balkans.
day_highlights: White Angel fresco at Mileševa | Crossing the Sandžak | Border into Montenegro | Highest town in the Balkans
day_note: Long transit day. Altitude — drink water.

subtitle: Kotor to Sarajevo Along the Coast in 6 Days
about: Six days, four old towns, three countries, and one of the most beautiful drives in Europe. This journey starts in the walled bay of Kotor, crosses Lovćen to the Croatian coast, walks Dubrovnik at first light, tastes the parent grape of Zinfandel on Pelješac, and ends at the rebuilt Mostar bridge lit up at the farewell dinner. | You will climb 1,355 steps above Kotor, sail to Our Lady of the Rocks, hear Diocletian's Roman palace explained by someone whose family has lived inside it for generations, and stand at Blagaj where a river emerges from a cliff face at the foot of a Sufi tekija. | Six days. The Adriatic, the way it should be done — slowly, before the cruise ships arrive.
inclusions: 5 nights accommodation (twin share) | Private vehicle and driver throughout | English-speaking tour leader | Daily breakfast | Welcome dinner in Kotor and farewell dinner in Mostar | Lunch in Perast | Pelješac winery tasting with food pairing | Boat tour of the Bay of Kotor | Kotor and Dubrovnik wall tickets | All entry fees per itinerary | Local specialist guides in Kotor, Dubrovnik, Split, and Mostar | Maximum 8 guests | Free cancellation up to 60 days before
exclusions: International flights | Travel insurance — mandatory | Lunches except where specified | Personal expenses | Gratuities

day_summary: The fortress climb at first light then a boat tour of the bay.

day_morning: 08:30 climb the city walls to Saint John's fortress — 1,355 steps, 260m elevation. Best done early before heat.
day_afternoon: 11:00 boat tour of the Bay of Kotor — Perast and Our Lady of the Rocks. Light lunch in Perast. Afternoon walking tour: Cathedral of Saint Tryphon (1166), Maritime Museum, the Venetian lions on every gate.
day_highlights: Saint John's fortress climb | Bay of Kotor boat tour | Our Lady of the Rocks | Cathedral of Saint Tryphon
day_note: Wall climb in heat is dangerous — early start non-negotiable in summer.

day_summary: Twenty-five serpentines to the Njegoš mausoleum then north to Dubrovnik.
day_included_activities: Lovćen 25 serpentines | Njegoš Mausoleum (1,657m) | Sveti Stefan photo stop
day_optional_activities: Budva beach stop
day_morning: 09:00 drive the 25 hairpin serpentines up to Lovćen National Park. 10:30 Njegoš Mausoleum at 1,657m. Panoramic view across to Albania.
day_afternoon: 13:00 lunch in Cetinje. 15:00 photo stop at Sveti Stefan and Budva. 17:30 cross into Croatia. 19:00 arrive Dubrovnik.
day_highlights: Lovćen 25 serpentines | Njegoš Mausoleum at 1,657m | Sveti Stefan photo stop | Border into Croatia
day_note: Serpentines plus motion sickness — sit forward, ginger candy onboard.

day_morning: 08:00 walk the city walls — 1.94 km loop. Early start mandatory in summer.
day_afternoon: 10:30 old town walking tour — Stradun, Onofrio's fountains, Rector's Palace, Franciscan monastery. Free afternoon: ferry to Lokrum island or cable car up Srđ for sunset.

day_note: Walls in summer heat — early start essential. Bring water.

day_morning: 09:00 departure north. 10:30 Ston — the 5.5 km of medieval defensive walls and oysters from the bay below.

day_summary: Počitelj fortress, Blagaj tekija, Mostar bridge — and onward to Sarajevo.
day_included_activities: Počitelj cliff fortress | Blagaj Tekija | Stari Most tour | Farewell dinner over the lit bridge
day_optional_activities: Bridge diver watch — €25 | Late transfer to Sarajevo (2.5 hrs)
day_morning: 09:30 departure south. Cross back into Bosnia. 12:00 Počitelj cliff fortress.
day_afternoon: 14:30 Blagaj Tekija — the 16th-century Sufi dervish house at the source of the Buna river. 16:30 arrive Mostar. Walking tour of Stari Most — built 1566, destroyed 1993, rebuilt 2004. Farewell dinner overlooking the lit bridge. Late evening transfer to Sarajevo (130 km, 2.5 hrs) for next-day morning flights.
day_highlights: Počitelj cliff fortress | Blagaj Sufi tekija | Stari Most | Farewell dinner over the lit bridge
day_note: Sarajevo transfer late evening Day 6, or extend in Mostar by one night — €140 supplement.
